post.summary:
Initiates a bidirectional stream to receive messages for a specific
receiver. This stream implements the challenge-response handshake required
for receiver authentication before messages are delivered.
post.description:
Expected flow:
1. Client -> Server: ReceiveMessagesRequest(init = InitReceive{...})
2. Server -> Client: ReceiveMessagesResponse(challenge = Challenge{...})
3. Client -> Server: ReceiveMessagesRequest(auth_sig = AuthSignature{...})
4. Server -> Client: [Stream of ReceiveMessagesResponse(
message = MailboxMessage{...}
)]
5. Server -> Client: ReceiveMessagesResponse(eos = EndOfStream{}) or
ReceiveMessagesResponse(error = ReceiveError{...})

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Wrapper message for requests sent FROM the client TO the server during the
ReceiveMessages stream. (streaming inputs)

post.summary:
Sends a single message to a receiver's mailbox. Requires a valid, unused
Bitcoin transaction outpoint as proof of work/stake.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Request message for the SendMessage RPC.

authmailboxrpcAuthSignature.description: Carries the client's signature in response to the server's challenge.

raw_tx_data.description:
The raw Bitcoin transaction bytes, in standard Bitcoin serialization
format, containing the outpoint being claimed. The server will hash this
to get the TXID.

raw_block_header_data.description:
The raw block header bytes (typically 80 bytes) of the block in which the
transaction was mined. Contains the Merkle root against which the proof
is verified.

block_height.description:
The height at which the block was mined. This is used to determine the
block's validity and to ensure the transaction is not too old.

merkle_proof.description:
The Merkle path proving the transaction's inclusion in the block header's
Merkle root.

claimed_outpoint.description:
The specific output within the provided transaction being claimed as the
proof "token". The output at the given index must be a P2TR output.
The server must verify that the txid_hex matches the hash of the provided
transaction data, and that this specific outpoint index exists in the
transaction.

authmailboxrpcBitcoinMerkleInclusionProo.description:
Encapsulates the full proof required for sender authentication, demonstrating
that a specific transaction output was included in a Bitcoin block.

authmailboxrpcChallenge.description: Carries the challenge hash sent by the server to the client.

authmailboxrpcEndOfStream.description:
An empty message used to explicitly signal the normal end of the message
stream.

authmailboxrpcInitReceive.description: Carries the initial parameters from the client to start receiving messages.

message_id.description: The unique ID assigned to the message by the server upon storage.

encrypted_payload.description: The ECIES encrypted message payload, intended for the receiver.

sender_ephemeral_pubkey.description:
The sender's ephemeral public key used for the ECIES exchange. Required
by the receiver to derive the shared secret and decrypt the payload.

arrival_timestamp.description: Timestamp (Unix epoch seconds) when the message arrived at the server.

expiry_block_height.description:
Block height after which this message may be considered expired by the
system.

authmailboxrpcMailboxMessage.description: Represents a single message as stored and retrieved from the mailbox.

sibling_hashes.description:
List of sibling hashes in the Merkle path, ordered from the transaction's
sibling up towards the root. Each hash is typically 32 bytes.

bits.description:
The bitmask indicating the direction (left/right) of each sibling hash
in the Merkle tree. Each bit corresponds to a sibling hash in the
sibling_hashes list. 0 indicates left, 1 indicates right.

authmailboxrpcMerkleProof.description:
Represents the Merkle proof hashes needed to link a transaction to the Merkle
root within a Bitcoin block header.

txid_hex.description:
Transaction ID as a hex-encoded string (standard 64 characters).
While derivable from the raw tx in the proof, including it here makes
the claimed outpoint explicit.

server_time.description: The current server time in Unix epoch seconds.

authmailboxrpcPingResponse.description: Response message for the Ping RPC.

authmailboxrpcReceiveError.description: Represents a structured error encountered during the ReceiveMessages stream.

authmailboxrpcReceiveMessagesRequest.description:
Wrapper message for requests sent FROM the client TO the server during the
ReceiveMessages stream.

authmailboxrpcReceiveMessagesResponse.description:
Wrapper message for responses sent FROM the server TO the client
during the ReceiveMessages stream.

receiver_id.description:
The public key identifier of the intended receiver (ReceiverID), encoded
as the raw bytes of the compressed public key.

sender_ephemeral_pubkey.description: The sender's ephemeral public key used for ECIES.

tx_proof.description:
The Bitcoin Merkle Inclusion Proof used as the sender's
authentication. The server MUST perform full validation of this
proof:
1. Verify claimed_outpoint.txid_hex matches hash(raw_tx_data).
2. Verify claimed_outpoint.index is valid for the transaction.
3. Verify merkle_proof connects the transaction hash to the
   raw_block_header_data's Merkle root.
4. Verify block_header validity (e.g., PoW, potentially chain
   context).
5. Ensure the claimed_outpoint has not been used previously (check
   used_proofs table).

expiry_block_height.description: The block height after which this message may be considered expired.

authmailboxrpcSendMessageRequest.description: Request message for the SendMessage RPC.

message_id.description: The unique ID assigned to the stored message by the server.

authmailboxrpcSendMessageResponse.description: Response message for the SendMessage RPC.
